questions.questionText: Which one of the following properties is not shown by NO?
options: It combines with oxygen to form nitrogen dioxide
It is diamagnetic in gaseous state
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
Nitric oxide is paramagnetic in the gaseous state because of the presence of one unpaired electron in its outermost shell.

The electronic configuration of $$NO$$ is

$$\sigma _{1s}^2\,\sigma _{1s}^{ * 2}\,\sigma _{2s}^2\,\sigma _{2s}^{ \circ 2}\,\sigma _{{2_{{p_z}}}}^2\,\pi _{{2_{{p_x}}}}^2$$

$$ = \pi _{{2_{Py}}}^2\,\pi _{{2_{Px}}}^{ * 1}$$

questions.questionText: Heating white phosphorus with conc. NaOH solution gives mainly :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The gas produced by treating an aqueous solution of ammonium chloride with sodium nitrite is :
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
N2 gas is produced by treating an aqueous solution of ammonium chloride with sodium nitrite.

NH4Cl + NaNO2 → NaCl + N2 (g) ↑ + 2H2O

questions.questionText:
Concentrated hydrochloric acid when kept in open air sometimes produces a cloud of white fumes. The
explanation for it is that :
options: oxygen in air reacts with the emitted HCI gas to form a cloud of chlorine gas

strong affinity of HCI gas for moisture in air results in forming of droplets of liquid solution which appears
like a cloudy smoke

due to strong affinity for water, concentrated hydrochloric acid pulls moisture of air towards it self. This
moisture forms droplets of water and hence the cloud.
concentrated hydrochloric acid emits strongly smelling HCI gas all the time.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Dinitrogen is a robust compound, but reacts at high altitudes to form oxides. The oxide of nitrogen that can damage plant leaves and retard photosynthesis is :
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution: High concentration of NO2 can damage plant leaves and retard photosynthesis.

questions.questionText: Heating an aqueous solution of aluminium chloride to dryness will give :
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
The solution of aluminium chloride in water is acidic due to hydrolysis.

$$AlC{l_3} + 3{H_2}O\buildrel \, \over \longrightarrow Al{\left( {OH} \right)_3} + 3HCl.$$

On heating it till dryness $$Al{\left( {OH} \right)_3}$$ is converted into $$A{l_2}{O_3}$$

$$Al{\left( {OH} \right)_3}\buildrel \Delta \over \longrightarrow A{l_2}{O_3} + 3{H_2}O$$

questions.questionText: The noble gas that does not occur in the atmosphere is :
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution: Inert gas Radon(Ra) is not present in atmosphere. Remaining all the inert gas can be found in the atmosphere.

questions.questionText: Reaction of thionyl chloride with white phosphorus forms a compound [A], which on hydrolysis gives [B], a dibasic acid. [A] and [B] are respectively :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Evaluate the following statements related to group 14 elements for their correctness.

(A) Covalent radius decreases down the group from $$\mathrm{C}$$ to $$\mathrm{Pb}$$ in a regular manner.

(B) Electronegativity decreases from $$\mathrm{C}$$ to $$\mathrm{Pb}$$ down the group gradually.

(C) Maximum covalance of $$\mathrm{C}$$ is 4 whereas other elements can expand their covalance due to presence of d orbitals.

(D) Heavier elements do not form $$\mathrm{p} \pi-\mathrm{p} \pi$$ bonds.

(E) Carbon can exhibit negative oxidation states.

Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
options:
(A), (B) and (C) Only
(A) and (B) Only
(C), (D) and (E) Only
(C) and (D) Only
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
(A) Covalent radius of group 14 elements increases from $$\mathrm{C}$$ to $$\mathrm{Pb}$$.

(B) Electronegativity decreases from $$\mathrm{C}$$ to $$\mathrm{Si}$$ and thereafter it remains more or less constant.

(C) Maximum covalency of $$\mathrm{C}$$ is 4 as the outermost shell of $$\mathrm{C}$$ is $$2^{\text {nd }}$$ shell and it cannot expand its octet. But other elements can expand their covalency due to the availability of vacant d-orbitals.

(D) Heavier elements do not form $$\mathrm{p} \pi-\mathrm{p} \pi$$ bonds due to their larger atomic size.

(E) Carbon can show negative oxidation states when it is covalently bonded to less electronegative elements like $$\mathrm{CH}_4$$.

questions.questionText:
Given below are two statements : one is labelled as Assertion (A) and the other is labelled as Reason (R).

Assertion (A) : Both rhombic and monoclinic sulphur exist as $$\mathrm{S}_8$$ while oxygen exists as $$\mathrm{O}_2$$.

Reason (R) : Oxygen forms $$p \pi-p \pi$$ multiple bonds with itself and other elements having small size and high electronegativity like $$\mathrm{C}, \mathrm{N}$$, which is not possible for sulphur.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
options:
Both (A) and (R) are correct but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A)
(A) is correct but (R) is not correct
(A) is not correct but (R) is correct
Both $$(\mathbf{A})$$ and $$(\mathbf{R})$$ are correct and $$(\mathbf{R})$$ is the correct explanation of $$(\mathbf{A})$$
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
Oxygen exists as $$\mathrm{O}_2$$

Sulphur exists as $$\mathrm{S}_8$$

Because of small size of oxygen, oxygen can form $$2 \mathrm{p}_\pi-2 \mathrm{p}_\pi$$ bond.

$$\Rightarrow$$ Assertion (A) is correct.

$$\Rightarrow$$ Reason (R) is correct.

Reason (R) is correct explanation of Assertion (A).
